vulnerabilities.title: Centralized Minting Control
vulnerabilities.description: The contract owner has the ability to mint an unlimited amount of tokens to any address. This poses a significant risk of rug pull if the owner account is compromised or malicious.
vulnerabilities.impact: CRITICAL - Total supply dilution and value extraction.
vulnerabilities.likelihood: MEDIUM - Requires owner compromise or malice.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Transfer ownership to a multi-sig wallet or a governance contract with a timelock.

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Zero Address Validation
vulnerabilities.description: Functions like `transferOwnership` and `setMinter` do not validate if the new address is the zero address (0x0), which could lead to accidental loss of control.
vulnerabilities.impact: HIGH - Permanent loss of contract administrative rights.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement `require(newAddress != address(0))` checks in all administrative setter functions.

functions_analysis.name: mint(address to, uint256 amount)
functions_analysis.logic: Calculates total supply and checks for owner privilege. Lacks decentralization guards.

functions_analysis.name: transfer(address to, uint256 amount)
functions_analysis.logic: Standard ERC20 transfer logic. Correct balance subtraction and emission.

functions_analysis.name: burn(uint256 amount)
functions_analysis.logic: Reduces total supply and msg.sender balance. Implementation is standard.

questions.question: Is the SOV token owner intended to be a single EOA or a multi-sig?
Are the initial liquidity tokens locked in a verifiable vault?
